reachability_artifacts: real loop integration test showing the status bytes/revision before transition, the accepted revision in terminal/event output, and one conflict from a competing repository instance
sworn board or router command against an invalid status fixture recording a typed error and non-zero exit instead of an empty/planned state

effort_complexity.rationale: See spec.json; canonical revision bytes and portable atomic replacement require implementer confirmation before design review.

release_benefit: A SwornAgent operator can trust a recoverable autonomous release loop, receive durable attention pages, and monitor or safely control it from a mobile web board.

positive_scenarios: One writer advances a legal state and receives the new content revision.
Every runtime outcome/event names the revision it observed or created.
negative_scenarios: A stale process cannot overwrite a newer verdict.
Invalid or unreadable state cannot become planned, empty, or PASS.
validation.benefit_hypothesis: Revisioned validated writes eliminate lost updates and give commands, events, recovery, and web clients one concurrency token.

design_decisions.choice: Use a SHA-256 revision over authoritative validated record bytes rather than extend Baton slice-status-v1.
design_decisions.rationale: Cross-process optimistic concurrency is needed without forking the protocol schema.

design_decisions.choice: Make the loop the only runtime transition owner.
options: shared write library in every process
adapters submit commands to loop owner
design_decisions.human_decision: Ratified 2026-07-14 (D4): the local Sworn agent remains the final runtime-transition authority; adapters and hosted services submit commands for local authorization.
design_decisions.rationale: One owner prevents transport-specific transition semantics and races.
